commonProblems.h2: Common Nissan Engine Problems — What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: Nissan engines are dependable workhorses for daily commuting and light commercial use, but specific alliance-platform designs frequently encounter failure points that drive high-volume replacement searches. The 1.2 DIG-T petrol and 1.5 dCi diesel families account for the majority of UK failure queries, alongside turbo actuator degradation on 2.5 dCi units and CVT-linked thermal stress on urban crossovers. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components permanently engineered to address these original weak points — plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use the breakdown below to compare realistic UK repair tiers, weigh downtime against long-term value, and decide when a certified replacement delivers the most reliable return.

problems.h4: 1.2 DIG-T Timing Chain Failure – Why Nissan Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Nissan Qashqai, Juke, Note, Pulsar (2013–2019, HR12DD / H5FT)
problems.rootCause: Early-tensioner geometry and extended oil service intervals reduce upper-guide lubrication, causing rapid chain stretch, metallic top-end rattle, and eventual timing misalignment.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: | Replace stretched chain, upgrade tensioner and plastic guides, flush oil galleries | Only viable before metal shavings circulate. Extends life by 20,000–30,000 miles if maintained strictly. |

repairOptions.tier: | Intermediate – Engine-Out Timing & Oil Pump Service |
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: | Strip front engine cover, install heavy-duty chain assembly, replace high-pressure oil pump and guide rails | Suitable for mid-life engines. Requires rigorous short-interval servicing to avoid repeat stretch. |

| Supply & fit fully rebuilt unit with upgraded steel guides, reinforced tensioner, dyno-tested compression, and 12‑month warranty | Most cost-effective permanent solution. Eliminates chain anxiety and restores factory fuel economy. |

problems.recommendation: If your engine is only showing early rattle with no warning lights, a preventative chain and tensioner kit offers the best upfront value. Once you experience metal-on-metal noise, limp mode, or confirmed timing loss, internal debris has already compromised the sump and oil pump. We recommend a full reconditioned replacement in these cases — the specialist supply-and-fit cost remains significantly lower than dealership rebuilds, and the upgraded guides permanently resolve the factory weakness.
problems.cta: → Compare rebuilt HR12DD 1.2 DIG-T engine prices for your Nissan Qashqai or Juke

problems.h4: 1.5 dCi High Oil Consumption & Injector Wear – The Common Rail Weakness
problems.affectedModels: Nissan Qashqai, Juke, Micra, Note, NV200 Van (2006–2018, K9K / F9Q)
problems.rootCause: Carbon buildup on copper injector seals combined with early piston ring wear from frequent DPF regeneration cycles causes gradual oil burning, blue exhaust smoke, and compression drop.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: | Remove injectors, replace copper seals/washers, chemically decarbonise intake, reset ECU service timers | Stops minor consumption and delays major work by 15,000–20,000 miles. Not effective if compression is already low. |

repairOptions.tier: | Major – Short Block Rebuild & Piston Upgrade |
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: | Strip block, hone cylinders, install oversized pistons with heavy-duty rings, refinish crank bearings | Restores factory compression. Ideal for fleet vans and high-mileage family cars where chassis condition is excellent. |

| Supply & fit certified rebuilt long-block with updated piston rings, recalibrated common rail injectors, full bench test, and 12‑month warranty | Guarantees zero oil consumption on installation. Highest ROI for daily drivers and commercial operators facing MOT smoke tests. |

problems.recommendation: We advise injector seal replacement only if a compression test confirms cylinder walls are within tolerance. If your 1.5 dCi burns more than 1 litre per 1,000 miles, internal wear has progressed past surface-level repairs. A full reconditioned replacement with upgraded rings and pre-calibrated injectors eliminates repeat DPF clogging and provides immediate commuter reliability without dealership premium pricing.
problems.cta: → Get instant quotes for K9K 1.5 dCi replacement engines with uprated piston rings

problems.h4: 2.5 dCi Turbo Actuator & VNT Degradation – Performance Loss & Limp Mode
problems.affectedModels: Nissan X-Trail, Navara, Pathfinder, NP300 Pickup (2005–2020, YD25DDTi / TD25)
problems.rootCause: Exhaust soot accumulation and aggressive heat cycling cause variable nozzle turbine vanes to stick, overloading the electronic actuator and straining the turbo shaft bearings.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: | Replace faulty actuator motor, clean or replace stuck VNT vanes, recalibrate boost sensors and wiring | Restores power temporarily. High recurrence risk if intercooler piping leaks or EGR soot isn’t managed. |

| Fit remanufactured turbo core, replace intercooler hoses, flush oil feed/return lines, reset ECU boost maps | Solid mid-life solution. Best for owners planning 30,000+ more miles before trading or selling. |

repairOptions.tier: | Full Replacement – Reconditioned 2.5 dCi Engine & Turbo Match |
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: | Supply & fit fully rebuilt engine with pre-installed upgraded turbo/actuator, reinforced oil feed routing, CVT health check, and 12‑month warranty | Eliminates cascade failures. Essential for Navara/X-Trail owners towing heavy loads or running commercial routes. |

problems.recommendation: Actuator and vane cleaning is only worthwhile if the turbo shaft exhibits zero axial play. For engines with a history of boost leaks, black smoke, or frequent limp-mode activation, partial repairs rarely survive past 15,000 miles. We recommend a complete reconditioned engine paired with an upgraded turbo routing setup — the specialist all-in pricing undercuts main-dealer rebuilds by up to 40% while guaranteeing consistent towing performance.
problems.cta: → Compare YD25DDTi 2.5 dCi engine swap prices for your Nissan X-Trail or Navara

problems.group: CVT-Linked Engine Stress & Cooling System Failure
problems.h4: CVT-Linked Engine Stress & Cooling System Failure – Thermal Overload Risk
problems.affectedModels: Nissan Juke, Qashqai, Leaf e-Power, NV200 (2012–2020, MR20DD / HR16DE / EM57 Range Extender)
problems.rootCause: Degraded CVT fluid and torque converter shudder force the engine to sustain elevated RPMs during city driving, generating excess heat that warps aluminium cylinder heads and compromises head gasket integrity.

repairOptions.tier: | Minor – Cooling System Overhaul & Head Skim |
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: | Remove cylinder head, replace gasket, pressure-test block, flush cooling system, upgrade thermostat housing | Stops immediate overheating. Does not address the underlying CVT fluid degradation causing sustained thermal stress. |
| Re-machine head and block decks, install multi-layer steel gaskets, replace water pump, reinforce coolant routing | Restores thermal tolerance.
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable for low-mileage urban vehicles with documented transmission service history. |

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: | Supply & fit rebuilt engine with reinforced head bolts, upgraded cooling pump assembly, pre-matched torque converter check, and 12‑month warranty | Best long-term reliability for commuters. Eliminates hidden head micro-fractures and ensures seamless powertrain integration. |

problems.recommendation: Head gasket repairs on CVT-equipped Nissans rarely prevent repeat failure unless the transmission fluid was serviced within the last 40,000 miles. If diagnostic scans show persistent torque converter shudder or coolant contamination, we recommend bypassing partial repairs entirely. A reconditioned engine with pre-verified cooling upgrades paired with a certified transmission flush guarantees you avoid repeat downtime and maintain daily driver uptime at specialist pricing tiers.
problems.cta: → Check MR20DD / HR16DE replacement engine prices matched to your Nissan Juke or Qashqai

engineTypes.h2: Nissan Engine Types – Options, Costs & What to Expect
engineTypes.intro: Every Nissan engine type available through our UK network, with typical price ranges and recommendations based on your vehicle's age, condition and your budget.

types.title: Nissan Reconditioned Engines (Recon)
types.description: A stripped, cleaned and fully inspected engine where all worn components are replaced with OEM‑spec parts. Best for Nissan owners who want factory reliability without the factory price — ideal for Qashqai, Juke and Micra models where timing‑chain or injector wear is the primary concern.
types.priceRange: Typical price range: £1,300 – £3,800 (depending on engine code)

types.title: Nissan Rebuilt Engines
types.description: A complete rebuild with upgraded components addressing known failure points — such as reinforced timing chains for 1.2 DIG‑T units and uprated piston rings for 1.5 dCi Alliance engines — for a longer service life than a standard replacement.

types.title: Used Nissan Engines
types.description: A tested, low‑mileage take‑out unit from a donor vehicle. The most affordable option when budget is the priority, with verified history and warranty available — suitable for older Micra, Note or Almera models where a cost‑effective swap extends daily‑driver life.

types.title: Nissan Remanufactured Engines
types.description: A factory‑spec, as‑new engine: every surface is machined, every component is replaced, and the unit is built to meet exact OEM tolerances. The premium choice for performance models like GT‑R or Z, or owners wanting a zero‑mile engine for long‑term retention.

types.title: Nissan Refurbished Engines
types.description: A lightly serviced, cleaned, and tested engine that works reliably but hasn't had a complete rebuild. Often a pragmatic, fast solution for older or budget‑sensitive models like Primera, Sunny or Vanette where a full recon isn't economical.

types.title: Nissan Supply & Fit Engines
types.description: Your replacement engine delivered and professionally installed by a UK Nissan specialist. Includes removal of the old unit, fitting, fluids, and a workmanship warranty — removing the biggest barrier for commuters without a dedicated mechanic.
types.priceRange: Typical added cost: £400 – £900 on top of engine price
types.cta: Find Nissan engine supply & fit deals

engineTypes.closing: All engine types include a minimum 12‑month warranty. Prices are typical UK market ranges and depend on exact engine code, supplier, and any additional fitting services. Use our free comparison tool to get a tailored quote for your Nissan model today.

engineSizes.h2: Nissan Engines by Size – Find the Right Displacement for Your Model
engineSizes.intro: Every Nissan engine size explained — with the models and engine codes that used each displacement — so you can quickly identify the correct replacement unit for your vehicle.

items.title: Nissan 1.5L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.5L dCi is Nissan's highest-volume diesel displacement, powering the Qashqai, Juke, Note and NV200 across multiple Renault-Nissan alliance generations. These K9K-derived units deliver strong economy but commonly drive replacement searches due to oil consumption from piston ring wear and injector seal degradation on high-mileage commuter use
items.cta: Get quotes for Nissan 1.5L diesel engines
commonFailurePoints: High oil consumption from piston ring wear
copper injector seal leaks
DPF clogging on post-2015 Euro 6 variants

items.title: Nissan 1.6L Diesel Engines
items.description: Introduced to meet stricter emissions targets, the 1.6L dCi (R9M) appears in later Qashqai, Pulsar and NV300 applications. While more refined than the 1.5L, it shares alliance-platform vulnerabilities around EGR cooling and turbo actuation that frequently trigger replacement enquiries from fleet and private owners
items.cta: Get quotes for Nissan 1.6L diesel engines
commonFailurePoints: EGR cooler internal leaks
turbo actuator calibration faults
AdBlue dosing system failures on Euro 6 versions

items.title: Nissan 2.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.0L dCi (M9R) served as Nissan's mid-range diesel for larger crossovers and light commercials before the 2.3L twin-turbo arrived. Primarily found in X-Trail and Qashqai+ applications, these units are sought for replacement when timing chain tensioner wear or oil pump drive issues cause catastrophic bottom-end damage
items.cta: Get quotes for Nissan 2.0L diesel engines
commonFailurePoints: Timing chain tensioner fatigue
oil pump drive chain stretch
injector seat erosion on high-mileage examples

items.title: Nissan 2.2L Diesel Engines
items.description: An earlier-generation workhorse, the 2.2L dCi (YD22) powered first-gen X-Trail, Primera and Almera Tino models. While largely superseded, these engines still drive replacement demand from owners of ageing fleet vehicles where cylinder head cracking or injector seat failure has rendered repair uneconomical
items.cta: Get quotes for Nissan 2.2L diesel engines
commonFailurePoints: Cylinder head cracking under thermal stress
injector seat erosion causing compression loss
turbo oil feed coking

items.title: Nissan 2.3L Diesel Engines
items.description: The modern 2.3L twin-turbo dCi (YS23) powers the current Navara NP300 and NV400 commercial range. Designed for higher torque and towing capability, these complex units commonly trigger replacement searches when twin-turbo actuator synchronization faults or high-pressure fuel pump failures cause repeated limp-mode events
items.cta: Get quotes for Nissan 2.3L diesel engines
commonFailurePoints: Twin-turbo actuator synchronization faults
high-pressure fuel pump wear
EGR valve carbon buildup on urban-use vans

items.title: Nissan 2.5L Diesel Engines
items.description: Nissan's most versatile diesel displacement, the 2.5L dCi (YD25/TD25) has powered Navara pickups, Pathfinder SUVs and X-Trail crossovers for two decades. Replacement demand is consistently high due to turbo VNT vane sticking, timing chain guide fragmentation, and oil consumption on high-mileage commercial and off-road use
items.cta: Get quotes for Nissan 2.5L diesel engines
commonFailurePoints: Turbo VNT vane sticking causing boost loss
timing chain guide fragmentation
oil consumption from piston ring wear on 150k+ mile units

items.title: Nissan 3.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 3.0L dCi family includes the ZD30 for light commercials and the V9X for Pathfinder SUV applications. While robust when maintained, these larger diesels commonly drive replacement enquiries when piston cracking (ZD30 under load) or EGR cooler blockage (V9X) leads to catastrophic overheating or loss of compression
items.cta: Get quotes for Nissan 3.0L diesel engines
commonFailurePoints: ZD30 – piston cracking under heavy load
V9X – EGR cooler blockage causing overheating
turbo bearing wear on high-mileage towing applications

items.title: Nissan 1.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: Nissan's smallest petrol displacement, the 1.0L (BR10/CG10) powered early Micra and Pixo city cars. While simple and affordable to replace, these units commonly drive replacement searches when head gasket failure from overheating or distributor drive wear causes misfire and loss of compression in urban commuter use
items.cta: Compare Nissan 1.0L petrol engine prices
commonFailurePoints: Head gasket failure on overheated units
distributor drive gear wear causing timing drift
coolant leak at thermostat housing

items.title: Nissan 1.2L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.2L family includes naturally aspirated HR12DE, direct-injection HR12DD DIG-T, and supercharged H5FT variants powering Micra, Note, Juke and Qashqai. Replacement demand is dominated by early DIG-T units (pre-2017) where timing chain stretch from tensioner geometry issues causes rattle, misfire and potential valve interference
items.cta: Compare Nissan 1.2L petrol engine prices
commonFailurePoints: Timing chain stretch on pre-2017 DIG-T units
carbon buildup on intake valves (direct injection)
supercharger clutch wear on H5FT performance variants

items.title: Nissan 1.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.5L petrol (HR15DE) serves as an economical option in Note, Micra and NV200 applications where diesel isn't required. While generally reliable, these units commonly trigger replacement searches when variable valve timing solenoid clogging or coolant leaks at the thermostat housing cause persistent warning lights and reduced performance
items.cta: Compare Nissan 1.5L petrol engine prices
commonFailurePoints: VVT solenoid clogging causing rough idle
coolant leak at thermostat housing
ignition coil failure on high-mileage urban-use examples

items.title: Nissan 1.6L Petrol Engines
items.description: A versatile displacement spanning naturally aspirated HR16DE and turbocharged DIG-T (Z9M/Z9T) variants, the 1.6L powers Qashqai, Juke, Pulsar and serves as the generator engine in Leaf e-Power systems. Replacement demand centres on carbon buildup on turbo intake ports and CVT-linked thermal stress causing head gasket compromise in stop-start urban driving
items.cta: Compare Nissan 1.6L petrol engine prices
commonFailurePoints: Carbon buildup on turbo DIG-T intake ports
ignition coil failure on HR16
CVT-linked thermal stress causing head gasket compromise on urban-use vehicles

items.title: Nissan 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.0L family includes naturally aspirated MR20DE, direct-injection MR20DD and Renault-sourced M4R variants powering Qashqai, X-Trail and commercial applications. Replacement searches commonly stem from high-pressure fuel pump wear on DIG-T units or timing chain tensioner rattle on cold start for M4R applications
items.cta: Compare Nissan 2.0L petrol engine prices
commonFailurePoints: MR20DD – high-pressure fuel pump wear
carbon buildup on intake valves (direct injection)
M4R – timing chain tensioner rattle on cold start

items.title: Nissan 2.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.5L petrol (QR25/GR25) powered earlier X-Trail, Pathfinder and Qashqai+ models before turbo downsizing. While robust, these units commonly drive replacement enquiries when oil consumption from piston ring wear on early QR25 variants or supercharger coupler wear on the rare DER performance version causes persistent blue smoke or power loss
items.cta: Compare Nissan 2.5L petrol engine prices
commonFailurePoints: Oil consumption from piston ring wear on pre-2010 QR25
supercharger coupler wear on QR25DER
coolant leak at water pump housing on high-mileage examples

items.title: Nissan 3.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: Nissan's flagship V6 petrol, the 3.5L VQ35 family powered Pathfinder, Murano and 350Z models. Replacement demand is driven by oil gallery gasket failure on pre-2007 DE variants or timing chain guide wear on high-mileage HR versions – failures that typically warrant full replacement rather than partial repair on these performance-oriented applications
items.cta: Compare Nissan 3.5L petrol engine prices
commonFailurePoints: Oil gallery gasket failure on pre-2007 VQ35DE causing oil pressure loss
timing chain guide wear on high-mileage HR variants
coolant crossover pipe leaks

items.title: Nissan Electric Motors
items.description: Nissan's fully electric powertrains use synchronous AC traction motors (EM57 series) paired with lithium-ion battery packs in Leaf and e-NV200 applications. Motor failure is rare; replacement enquiries typically stem from inverter/resolver faults or battery degradation requiring specialist calibration and high-voltage safety protocols. System / motor code(s): EM57, EM48, EM60 (generation variants)
items.cta: Enquire about Nissan electric motor repair

items.title: Nissan 1.2L Hybrid Engines
items.description: Nissan's e-Power series hybrid uses a 1.2L HR12DD petrol engine solely as a generator to charge the battery, while an EM57 electric motor drives the wheels. Replacement searches focus on the generator engine where oil change neglect or carbon buildup causes reduced charging efficiency, requiring specialist calibration of the generator-motor control strategy. System / motor code(s): HR12DD (generator) + EM57 (traction motor)
items.cta: Enquire about Nissan 1.2L hybrid engine availability

engineCodeDirectory.h2: Nissan Engine Codes – Most Replaced Engines (2008–2025)
engineCodeDirectory.intro: Every major Nissan engine code from the last two decades, grouped by family, with compatible models and typical reasons why owners seek replacement. Select your engine to compare rebuilt prices and get quotes from UK Nissan specialists.

entries.title: K9K 832 1.5L Diesel
entries.description: Fitted to the Qashqai J10/J11, Juke F15 and Note E11 between 2008 and 2020, this high-volume Alliance diesel is the most searched Nissan replacement unit in the UK. Piston ring wear and DPF clogging from urban stop-start driving are the primary failure triggers. Quality rebuilt units feature reinforced piston rings, revised oil pump assemblies and MOT-compliant EGR routing.
entries.cta: Get quotes for K9K 832 Nissan engine replacement

entries.title: S9M 836 1.5L Diesel
entries.description: Used in the Juke F15, Note E12 and Micra K14 from 2010 to 2020, this updated 1.5L variant improved efficiency but retained susceptibility to injector seal leaks and turbo actuator sticking. When seals degrade, oil contamination triggers warning lights and eventual limp mode. Rebuilt replacements include upgraded injector seals, remanufactured VGT actuators and flow-tested oil rails.

entries.title: F9Q 772 1.5L Diesel
entries.description: Found in the Almera N16, Cube Z12 and Kubistar from 2002 to 2012, this legacy 1.5L unit powers entry-level commuters and light vans. Crankshaft bearing spin and chronic DPF regeneration failure are the dominant replacement drivers. Supply-ready units include uprated crankshaft bearings, EGR/MOT-compliant DPF assemblies and calibrated fuel mapping.
entries.cta: Request rebuilt F9Q 772 Nissan engine quotes

entries.title: R9M 832 1.6L Diesel
entries.description: Standard across the Qashqai J11, Pulsar C13 and NV300 commercial vans from 2014 to 2021, this efficient Euro 6 diesel suffers from EGR cooler cracking and differential pressure sensor faults. Coolant ingress into the intake manifold causes misfires and potential hydro-lock. Rebuilt units replace the cooler core with reinforced alloys and install updated DPF pressure sensors.
entries.cta: Find supply & fit for R9M 832 Nissan engines

entries.title: M9R 730 2.0L Diesel
entries.description: Installed in the Qashqai J10, X-Trail T31 and NV400 from 2006 to 2018, this torque-rich 2.0L commercial crossover engine is prone to timing chain tensioner collapse and high-pressure fuel pump wear. Sudden tensioner failure can lead to catastrophic valve damage. Replacements feature upgraded hydraulic tensioners, precision-calibrated HPFPs and reinforced chain guides.
entries.cta: Get quotes for M9R 730 Nissan engine swap

entries.title: YD25DDTi 2.5L Diesel
entries.description: The workhorse of the Navara D40/D23, X-Trail T31 and Pathfinder R51 (2005–2021), this 2.5L diesel powers 4x4s and fleet vehicles across tough UK conditions. Turbo vane carbon buildup and injector nozzle coking cause severe power loss and excessive black smoke. Rebuilt units include upgraded variable geometry turbos, flow-tested injectors and reinforced intercooler piping.
entries.cta: Compare YD25DDTi Nissan engine prices UK

entries.title: ZD30DDTi 3.0L Diesel
entries.description: Equipping the Cabstar F24, Interstar and NT400 vans from 2000 to 2018, this 3.0L workhorse is built for heavy payloads but suffers piston cracking under sustained high-RPM loads. EGR valve failure and turbo centre-section degradation accelerate downtime for commercial operators. Replacements use forged reinforced pistons, revised EGR cooler assemblies and heavy-duty oil pans.
entries.cta: Get quotes for ZD30DDTi Nissan commercial engines

entries.title: YD22DDTi 2.2L Diesel
entries.description: Common in the Almera N16, Primera P12 and early X-Trail T30 (2001–2007), this mid-range diesel bridges the gap between 1.5L and 2.5L platforms. Turbo actuator linkage failure and glow plug relay burnout are the most frequent catastrophic triggers. Rebuilt units feature remanufactured actuator linkages, heavy-duty relays and upgraded turbo wastegate valves.

entries.title: HR12DD 1.2L SC Petrol
entries.description: Fitted to the Note E12, Juke F15 and Micra K14 from 2010 to 2019, this supercharged 1.2L unit delivers strong economy but is notorious for timing chain stretch accelerated by fuel dilution. Chain rattle on cold starts precedes complete tensioner failure and valve interference. Rebuilt engines install heavy-duty timing chains, reinforced tensioners and revised PCV ventilation systems.
entries.cta: Request rebuilt HR12DD Nissan engine quotes

entries.title: H5Bt 415 1.2L Turbo Petrol
entries.description: Powering the Qashqai J11 and Pulsar C13 between 2014 and 2020, this turbocharged variant improves mid-range torque but accumulates severe carbon deposits on direct injection valves. Pre-ignition knock and misfire codes follow unchecked buildup. Replacements include decarbonised intake ports, reinforced forged pistons and updated ECU anti-knock mapping.
entries.cta: Find supply & fit for H5Bt 411 Nissan engines

entries.title: Z9M 1.6L Turbo Petrol
entries.description: Available in the Pulsar C13, Qashqai J11 and X-Trail T32 (2015–2022), this 1.6L turbo petrol balances performance with family-car practicality. Turbo bearing wear and intercooler hose degradation under high boost cause sudden power loss and oil leaks. Rebuilt units feature upgraded turbo bearing housings, silicone-reinforced charge pipes and calibrated wastegates.
entries.cta: Get quotes for Z9M 1.6 Nissan engine replacement

entries.title: MR20DD 2.0L Petrol
entries.description: The standard engine for the Qashqai J11, X-Trail T32 and NV300 vans from 2012 to 2022. Valve stem seal hardening causes excessive blue smoke on startup, while early timing guides wear prematurely. Rebuilt units install high-temperature silicone valve stem seals, reinforced nylon timing guides and updated cam phasers.

entries.title: QR25DE 2.5L Petrol
entries.description: Found in the X-Trail T30/T31 and Pathfinder R51 (2005–2015), this robust 2.5L inline-four powers larger crossovers and SUVs. Oil pump drive failure and head gasket warping under thermal stress are the leading replacement causes. Replacements include high-flow oil pump assemblies, multi-layer steel (MLS) head gaskets and upgraded cooling thermostats.
entries.cta: Request rebuilt QR25DE Nissan engine quotes

entries.title: HR15DE 1.5L Petrol
entries.description: Fitted to the Micra K13, Note E11 and Tiida C11 from 2005 to 2016. Ignition coil pack failure and valve cover gasket leaks are the most common catastrophic triggers for urban commuters. Rebuilt units feature high-performance coil-on-plug assemblies, reinforced silicone valve covers and updated spark plug gaskets.
entries.cta: Get quotes for HR15DE Nissan engine swap

entries.title: VQ35HR 3.5L V6 Petrol
entries.description: Powering the 350Z Z34, Pathfinder R51 and Murano Z51 (2007–2015), this high-revving V6 is prized for smoothness but suffers timing chain guide degradation and oil gallery sludge. Chain slack leads to rattling and eventual cam timing loss. Rebuilt engines use premium reinforced chain guides, precision-balanced crankshaft assemblies and full oil gallery cleaning.
entries.cta: Compare VQ35HR Nissan V6 engine prices

entries.title: EM57 1.6L Hybrid
entries.description: Generator Exclusive to the Note E12 e-Power and Serena C27 (2016–2022), this 1.6L series-hybrid generator charges the traction battery while the electric motor drives the wheels. Generator bearing wear and inverter thermal stress cause charging interruptions and fault codes. Replacements include reinforced generator bearings, upgraded inverter cooling modules and calibrated voltage regulators.
entries.cta: Find EM57 Nissan hybrid generator quotes

entries.title: TD27 2.7L Diesel
entries.description: Used in the Terrano II R20, Patrol Y60 and Vanette from 1995 to 2005. Injector pump timing drift and coolant system seal degradation are the primary failure points for these ageing off-road and commercial units. Rebuilt units feature remanufactured Bosch injection pumps, upgraded coolant system seals and reinforced head bolts.
entries.cta: Get quotes for TD27 Nissan engine replacement

entries.title: NA16S 1.6L Petrol
entries.description: Equipped in the Sunny N14 and Almera N15 (1990–2000). Distributor cap wear and camshaft lobe scoring cause misfires and rough idling on these classic entry-level models. Replacements include upgraded electronic distributor assemblies, hardened camshaft followers and renewed valve train components. Closing line (Block A): Can’t find your engine code listed above? engineYears.h2: Nissan Engines by Year – What Was Fitted & When

years.preview: SR20 & VQ-Series Launch: Performance Foundation Era Major Engine Family Introduction
years.description: The early 1990s delivered two of Nissan's most enduring engine families - the SR20DET turbocharged four-cylinder and the VQ-series V6 - establishing the performance and refinement pillars of every Nissan model line that followed for three decades.
keyChanges: SR20DE / SR20DET 2.0-litre four-cylinder introduced from 1990 - DOHC construction, optional turbocharger, and a tuning potential that made it one of the most modified Nissan engines in UK aftermarket history
VQ30DE 3.0-litre V6 introduced 1994 across Maxima and early Skyline variants - smooth, free-revving and refined; basis for the VQ family that runs to the present day
KA24DE 2.4-litre four-cylinder continues in Pickup/Terrano applications - a robust, low-tech unit appreciated for its longevity in working vehicle use
RB26DETT 2.6-litre twin-turbo inline-six continues in R32/R33 Skyline GT-R - the most celebrated Nissan performance engine of the era
QG16DE / QG18DE 1.6 and 1.8-litre four-cylinder introduced from 1999 in Almera and Primera - modern replacements for the GA-series in volume passenger cars
mainEngines: SR20DE / SR20DET - 2.0-litre DOHC four-cylinder (naturally aspirated / turbocharged) (Nissan Primera, Almera, 200SX S13/S14/S15)
VQ30DE - 3.0-litre V6 (Nissan Maxima, Nissan Skyline)
RB26DETT - 2.6-litre twin-turbo inline-six (Nissan Skyline GT-R R32, R33, R34)
KA24DE - 2.4-litre four-cylinder (Nissan Pathfinder, Terrano, Pickup)
QG16DE / QG18DE - 1.6 / 1.8-litre DOHC four-cylinder (Nissan Almera N15/N16, Primera)
popularModels: Nissan Skyline GT-R R32 / R33 / R34
knownFor: SR20DET is the definitive UK drift and track-day four-cylinder - robust under forced induction but oil pump drive chain wear and oil starvation on hard cornering are documented risks on worn or unmodified units
RB26DETT is a heritage landmark engine - strong bottom-end but turbo oil feed lines and IACV valves are the primary maintenance targets on surviving examples
QG18DE in the Almera is a proven, low-drama workhorse; timing chain service is the primary long-term maintenance requirement
SR20DE naturally aspirated is a dependable commuter engine; direct replacement with SR20DET is a common UK upgrade path, making engine identification critical when sourcing replacements
years.ticker: Common Replacement Enquiries: Nissan 200SX SR20DET engine replacement UK - Nissan SR20DET rebuilt engine for sale - Nissan Skyline GT-R RB26DETT engine replacement - Nissan Primera 2.0 SR20 engine rebuild
years.cta: Search for 1990–2002 Nissan SR20, RB26 and VQ replacement engines - compare UK specialist rebuilt and remanufactured options

years.preview: VQ35DE, Z350 & Primera: V6 Performance Golden Age Performance Landmark
years.description: The early 2000s saw Nissan's VQ35DE 3.5-litre V6 become the engine around which an entire performance model range was built - powering the 350Z, Murano and Pathfinder, and cementing the VQ family's reputation as one of the finest naturally aspirated V6 architectures of its era.
keyChanges: VQ35DE 3.5-litre V6 introduced from 2000 - a development of the VQ30, producing 280 PS in the 350Z; winner of Ward's 10 Best Engines for multiple consecutive years
Nissan 350Z launched UK 2003 with VQ35DE as sole engine option - the first pure Nissan sports car in UK showrooms since the 200SX
VQ35HR high-revving variant introduced from 2006 in the 350Z facelift - revised intake and exhaust cam timing, raising output to 313 PS
MR20DE 2.0-litre four-cylinder introduced in Qashqai Mk1 and X-Trail T31 from 2006–2007 - Nissan's new mainstream petrol unit
QR25DE 2.5-litre four-cylinder replaces older units in Altima, X-Trail petrol
mainEngines: VQ35DE - 3.5-litre V6 (Nissan 350Z Z33, Nissan Murano, Nissan Pathfinder R51)
VQ35HR - 3.5-litre V6 high-revving (Nissan 350Z facelift 2006+)
MR20DE - 2.0-litre DOHC four-cylinder (Nissan Qashqai J10, Nissan X-Trail T31)
QR25DE - 2.5-litre four-cylinder (Nissan Altima, X-Trail petrol)
knownFor: VQ35DE is widely regarded as one of the finest naturally aspirated V6 production engines ever made; oil consumption from worn valve stem seals is the primary known issue at high mileage
350Z VQ35DE big-end bearing wear at high revs is documented - particularly on examples that have been used for track days without regular oil changes; a significant cost if not caught early
MR20DE in the Qashqai J10 is a reliable and understated unit; timing chain tensioner wear at high mileage is the standard preventative advice from UK specialists
VQ35HR brings noticeably higher revving capability over the DE variant; the same valve stem sealing concern applies, though somewhat less pronounced in normal road use
years.ticker: Common Replacement Enquiries: Nissan 350Z VQ35DE engine replacement UK - Nissan 350Z engine rebuild big end bearing - Nissan Qashqai 2.0 MR20 engine for sale - VQ35 rebuilt engine UK
years.cta: Compare 2000–2007 Nissan VQ35 and MR20 replacement engines - specialist rebuilt options available for 350Z, Qashqai and X-Trail

years.preview: Qashqai J10, Juke & Diesel Dominance: 1.5 dCi & 2.0 dCi Era
years.description: The launch of the first-generation Qashqai in 2006 and the Juke in 2010 created Nissan's highest-volume UK engine demand around the 1.5 dCi and 2.0 dCi Renault-sourced diesel units - engines whose reliability record in real-world UK stop-start use is among the most discussed of any Nissan engine family.
keyChanges: Renault-sourced 1.5 dCi (K9K) introduced as the primary entry diesel across Qashqai J10, Juke and Micra - a proven, widely deployed unit requiring strict timing belt replacement intervals
Renault-sourced 2.0 dCi (M9R) introduced in Qashqai J10 and X-Trail T31 - a more capable diesel producing up to 150 PS but sensitive to DPF blockage in short-trip UK use
1.6-litre DiG-T (HR16DE turbo / H4Bt) four-cylinder petrol introduced in Juke from 2011 - Nissan's first turbocharged small petrol in the UK mass market
Nissan Micra K12 continues with HR10DE / HR16DE 1.0 and 1.6 naturally aspirated - a simple, reliable entry-level powertrain
Euro 5 compliance across UK lineup from 2011 - DPF fitment standardised on all diesel variants
mainEngines: K9K (1.5 dCi) - 1.5-litre turbocharged diesel (Nissan Qashqai J10, Juke, Micra K13, Note)
M9R (2.0 dCi) - 2.0-litre turbocharged diesel (Nissan Qashqai J10, X-Trail T31)
HR16DE - 1.6-litre naturally aspirated four-cylinder (Nissan Micra K12, Note, Juke base)
HR16DE Turbo (DiG-T) - 1.6-litre turbocharged petrol (Nissan Juke, Nissan Note NISMO)
MR20DE - 2.0-litre petrol four-cylinder (Nissan Qashqai J10 petrol continues)
knownFor: Renault K9K 1.5 dCi timing belt must be replaced at 60,000-mile / 4-year intervals - failure to do so is the single most common cause of catastrophic engine failure across the Qashqai and Juke fleet
M9R 2.0 dCi is susceptible to DPF blockage in short-trip UK driving; combined with turbo failures at high mileage, it generates significant replacement demand among Qashqai J10 owners
1.6 DiG-T in the Juke is a willing and entertaining petrol unit but the turbo oil feed pipe blockage causing turbo failure is a well-documented and preventable failure point
K9K 1.5 dCi is a broadly reliable diesel when correctly serviced; its widespread use across Renault and Nissan makes parts supply excellent and independent specialist labour rates competitive
years.ticker: Common Replacement Enquiries: Nissan Qashqai 1.5 dCi engine replacement - Nissan Qashqai 2.0 dCi engine for sale UK - Nissan Juke 1.6 DiG-T engine replacement - Nissan X-Trail 2.0 dCi M9R engine - Nissan Juke diesel engine problems UK
years.cta: Get specialist quotes for 2006–2014 Nissan Qashqai, Juke and X-Trail 1.5 dCi and 2.0 dCi replacement engines - compare UK rebuilt diesel options now

years.preview: GT-R R35: VR38DETT & Ongoing Performance Legacy Performance Landmark
years.description: The R35 Nissan GT-R launched in 2008 in the UK carrying the all-new VR38DETT - a 3.8-litre twin-turbocharged V6 hand-assembled by a single Takumi craftsman, producing supercar performance from a production engine that has remained in continuous evolution and active UK road use ever since.
keyChanges: VR38DETT 3.8-litre twin-turbo V6 introduced in Nissan GT-R R35 - 480 PS at launch (2008 UK), rising to 570 PS in Nismo spec (2014) and 600 PS in Nismo N-Attack Package
Each VR38DETT hand-assembled by a single Takumi technician; engine serial number and builder's plaque fitted as standard
Variable valve timing on both intake and exhaust camshafts from launch - a sophisticated system given the extreme output targets
Dry-sump lubrication ensures consistent oil supply under extreme lateral cornering forces - the GT-R's track capability is partly dependent on this system's integrity
2017 MY revision introduced updated turbochargers and combustion refinements; final GT-R production run confirmed from 2023 onward as the model approaches end-of-life
mainEngines: VR38DETT - 3.8-litre twin-turbo V6 (Nissan GT-R R35 Standard / Black Edition / Track Edition)
VR38DETT Nismo - 3.8-litre twin-turbo V6 uprated (Nissan GT-R Nismo 570 PS / 600 PS N-Attack)
knownFor: VR38DETT is a precision-engineered masterpiece but extremely sensitive to lubrication quality - any lapse in oil temperature management or incorrect fluid spec risks bearing failure; specialist GT-R rebuilders in the UK command premium rates for good reason
Transmission and differential failures on track-used examples generate almost as much replacement demand as engine issues; specialists advise keeping the two services linked
VR38DETT rebuild is a highly specialised operation - only a handful of UK workshops carry the required tooling and Nissan's own GT-R dealer network is the benchmark for rebuild quality
As R35 GT-R production ends and examples age, heritage-grade VR38DETT replacement demand will only increase - early sourcing of reliable rebuilt units is strongly advised
years.ticker: Common Replacement Enquiries: Nissan GT-R VR38DETT engine replacement UK - Nissan GT-R R35 engine rebuild specialist - VR38DETT rebuilt engine for sale - Nissan GT-R Nismo engine replacement
years.cta: Source a precision-rebuilt VR38DETT for your Nissan GT-R R35 - compare UK GT-R specialist engine quotes with 12-month warranty

years.preview: 370Z, VQ37VHR & Navara Diesel: NA V6 Peak & Pickup Diesel Entry
years.description: The VQ37VHR 3.7-litre high-revving V6 in the Nissan 370Z represented the pinnacle of the naturally aspirated VQ family - while simultaneously the Navara D40 and D231 were driving significant diesel replacement demand around the 2.5 dCi YD25 unit in the pickup truck sector.
keyChanges: VQ37VHR 3.7-litre V6 introduced in Nissan 370Z from 2009 UK - VVEL (Variable Valve Event and Lift) system producing 328 PS, the highest output naturally aspirated VQ ever
Navara D40 continues with 2.5 dCi (YD25DDTi) - a Renault-derived diesel producing 174 PS but the subject of significant UK DPF and EGR complaints under stop-start use
Nissan Pathfinder R51 transitions from 2.5 dCi to available 3.0 dCi (V9X) V6 diesel from 2010 - a more capable but more complex unit
1.6 dCi (R9M) introduced in Pulsar and later Qashqai J11 from 2014 - a more Euro 5/6-capable diesel than the K9K for mid-size applications
HR15DE 1.5-litre naturally aspirated introduced in Micra K13 from 2011 - a simple, low-cost unit optimised for city use
mainEngines: VQ37VHR - 3.7-litre V6 VVEL (Nissan 370Z, Infiniti G37)
YD25DDTi - 2.5-litre turbocharged diesel (Nissan Navara D40, Nissan Pathfinder R51)
V9X - 3.0-litre V6 twin-turbo diesel (Nissan Pathfinder R51 top-spec)
R9M (1.6 dCi) - 1.6-litre turbocharged diesel (Nissan Pulsar, Qashqai J11 from 2014)
HR15DE - 1.5-litre naturally aspirated (Nissan Micra K13)
knownFor: VQ37VHR is a high-revving masterpiece; VVEL actuator failures are a known and expensive issue on higher-mileage examples - a critical inspection point when sourcing a replacement engine

Navara D40 YD25DDTi is a robust diesel for its class but DPF blockage and EGR fouling are persistent concerns in UK-duty stop-start use; head gasket failure at very high mileage is also documented on the 2.5 dCi

370Z VQ37VHR big-end bearings can show wear on examples used hard without strict oil change adherence - the same recommendation as for the VQ35DE applies: treat it as a performance engine and service accordingly
R9M 1.6 dCi introduced measurably better real-world fuel economy in the Qashqai J11 over the outgoing K9K but DPF sensitivity in urban use remains a concern
years.ticker: Common Replacement Enquiries: Nissan 370Z VQ37 engine replacement UK - Nissan Navara 2.5 dCi engine replacement - Nissan Navara YD25 engine for sale - Nissan 370Z VVEL failure engine rebuild
years.cta: Compare 2009–2015 Nissan 370Z and Navara replacement engines - UK specialists for VQ37VHR and YD25 diesel rebuilds

years.preview: Qashqai J11 & Juke F16: 1.2 DIG-T, 1.3 DIG-T & Timing Chain Crisis Major Reliability Revision
years.description: The second-generation Qashqai J11 and Juke F16 introduced the Renault-Nissan Alliance 1.2-litre DIG-T and its successor the 1.3-litre DIG-T - engines that brought improved efficiency but generated the most widespread timing chain replacement demand of any Nissan petrol in UK history.
keyChanges: H5Ft (1.2-litre DIG-T turbo) introduced in Qashqai J11 and Juke F15 from 2013 - a Renault-Nissan Alliance unit shared with Renault Clio and Captur
Timing chain wear on the H5Ft 1.2 DIG-T is the dominant UK failure - soot agglomeration in the oil accelerates chain thinning and VVT sprocket wear, often from 60,000–80,000 miles
HR10DE 1.0-litre naturally aspirated three-cylinder introduced in Micra K14 from 2017 - a simple, three-pot unit without the chain concerns of the DIG-T
1.3-litre H5H / H5Ht DIG-T introduced in Qashqai J11 facelift (2018) and Juke F16 (2019) - replacing the 1.2 and addressing the most critical timing chain concerns with a revised design
1.5 dCi K9K continues in Qashqai J11 diesel; Euro 6-compliant from 2015 update
Nissan Navara NP300 D23 launched 2016 with 2.3 dCi (M9T) twin-turbo diesel - replacing YD25 and matching the Renault Master / Mercedes Sprinter diesel family
mainEngines: H5Ft (1.2 DIG-T) - 1.2-litre turbocharged petrol four-cylinder (Nissan Qashqai J11, Juke F15, Pulsar)
H5H / H5Ht (1.3 DIG-T) - 1.3-litre turbocharged petrol (Nissan Qashqai J11 facelift, Juke F16, Nissan Micra K14 DIG-T)
K9K (1.5 dCi Euro 6) - 1.5-litre diesel continues (Qashqai J11 diesel)
M9T (2.3 dCi) - 2.3-litre twin-turbo diesel (Nissan Navara NP300, Nissan NV400)
HR10DE - 1.0-litre three-cylinder naturally aspirated (Nissan Micra K14 base)
knownFor: H5Ft 1.2 DIG-T timing chain failure is the single most searched Nissan engine fault in the UK - chain wear from oil soot agglomeration causes rattling on cold start, VVT sprocket collapse, and ultimately catastrophic engine failure if ignored

H5H 1.3 DIG-T addresses the worst of the 1.2's issues but is not entirely immune; strict oil change intervals using the correct low-SAPS specification oil are the critical maintenance instruction from UK specialists
Navara NP300 2.3 dCi M9T is a substantial improvement over the YD25 diesel - powerful, refined and broadly reliable, but DPF and EGR maintenance remain the primary service discipline in UK use

faq.h2: Nissan Engine Replacement – Frequently Asked Questions
faq.intro: Quick, honest answers to the most common questions Nissan owners ask before comparing engine replacement prices and finding a UK specialist.

items.question: How much does a Nissan engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A reconditioned Nissan engine typically costs £1,800–£4,500 depending on displacement and complexity, with small 1.2L–1.5L units at the lower end and 2.5L–3.5L commercial or performance engines at the higher range. Supply‑and‑fit labour adds £400–£900, while main dealers often charge 30–50% more for equivalent work. For budget‑conscious owners, verified used units start around £800–£2,500 but carry shorter warranties. We always recommend comparing at least three specialist quotes to secure the best value for your specific engine code.
items.cta: Compare Nissan engine prices now

items.question: What affects the cost of a Nissan engine replacement?
items.answer: Four key factors drive pricing: engine size (1.2L DIG‑T vs 3.5L VQ35), condition (used vs reconditioned vs remanufactured), vehicle complexity (FWD Qashqai vs AWD Navara), and whether you choose supply‑only or full supply‑and‑fit. Location matters too—London and South East labour rates can be 20–30% higher than regional specialists. Our comparison tool provides transparent, postcode‑matched quotes so you see exactly what you're paying for before committing.
items.cta: Compare Nissan engine prices now

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine in my Nissan?
items.answer: In most cases, yes—especially if the chassis, gearbox, and interior remain in good condition. A rebuilt Nissan engine with upgraded components (e.g., reinforced timing guides on HR12DD units or uprated piston rings on K9K 1.5 dCi) often outlasts the original factory specification. The alternative—buying a replacement vehicle—typically costs significantly more than a specialist‑supplied reconditioned powertrain. If your Qashqai, Navara or Leaf holds sensible market value, engine replacement is almost always the smarter financial decision.
items.cta: Get a Nissan engine quote now

items.question: What's included in a Nissan engine supply and fit service?
items.answer: A full supply‑and‑fit package covers sourcing the correct engine code, removing the failed unit, installing the replacement with new gaskets and fluids, resetting ECU adaptations, and conducting a post‑fit test drive. Most specialists also include disposal of the old engine and a workmanship warranty alongside the engine warranty. Fitting costs typically range £400–£900 depending on model accessibility—always confirm whether VAT, delivery and ancillary routing are included before booking.
items.cta: Get a Nissan engine quote now

items.question: How long does a Nissan engine replacement take?
items.answer: For common models like Qashqai, Juke or Note with widely stocked codes (K9K, HR12DD, MR20DD), a like‑for‑like swap typically takes 3–5 working days from order to road‑ready. More complex applications—Navara 2.5 dCi, Pathfinder V6, or Leaf e‑Power systems—may require 5–7 days due to specialist calibration or hybrid system verification. The biggest variable is engine availability; our network prioritises high‑demand codes to minimise downtime. Always ask suppliers about current lead times when requesting your quote.
items.cta: Get a Nissan engine quote now

items.question: Do reconditioned Nissan engines come with warranty?
items.answer: Yes—every reconditioned Nissan engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty as standard. Many specialists also offer extended 24‑month coverage on fully rebuilt or remanufactured units. Warranty protection typically covers major internal components: crankshaft, pistons, timing chain/belt, oil pump and cylinder head. We always advise confirming exact terms, exclusions and claim procedures directly with your chosen supplier before finalising your order.
items.cta: Find Nissan engines with warranty

items.question: Should I choose a used or reconditioned Nissan engine?
items.answer: Used Nissan engines (£800–£2,500) offer lower upfront cost and suit temporary fixes or vehicles being sold soon. Reconditioned units (£1,800–£4,500) include new bearings, seals, upgraded components addressing known failure points (e.g., steel timing guides on HR12DD), and a 12‑month warranty—typically delivering another 100k+ miles of reliable service. For failure‑prone families like the 1.2 DIG‑T or 1.5 dCi, we strongly recommend reconditioned with upgrades to avoid repeat downtime.

Simply enter your registration—our system matches you with nearby suppliers who stock your exact engine code and offer fitting services. items.question: What are the most common Nissan engine failures?
items.answer: The most frequent catastrophic failures driving replacement searches are: HR12DD 1.2 DIG‑T timing chain stretch (pre‑2017 Qashqai/Juke), K9K 1.5 dCi piston ring wear causing high oil consumption, YD25 2.5 dCi turbo VNT actuator sticking, MR20/HR16 petrol heads warped by CVT‑linked thermal stress, and post‑2014 diesel EGR/DPF blockages triggering MOT failure. Early warning signs rarely allow preventative repair—once metallic noise, blue smoke or persistent limp mode appears, full replacement is typically the most cost‑effective solution.

items.question: How can I get the best price on a Nissan engine?
items.answer: Compare multiple quotes—prices for the same engine code (e.g., K9K 1.5 dCi) can vary by £1,000+ across suppliers. Consider used units for tight budgets or short‑term ownership, but opt for reconditioned with upgraded components if you plan to keep the Nissan long‑term. Ask whether quotes include fitting, delivery, VAT and old‑engine disposal to avoid hidden costs. Finally, verify that the supplier can match your exact VIN/platform to prevent compatibility issues.
